revision.text:
[[ja:長者森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}} {{Ancient Japanese Kofuns |name = Chōjamori Kofun |image = [[File:Chojagamori Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance
(In the background: Former {{ill|Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|ja|福知山市立育英小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|1=Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594340}} building) |alternate_name = |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, Yakuno-chō Takauchi (Aza Asabatake)
(Former site of {{ill|Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|ja|福知山市立育英小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|1=Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594340}}) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.20 |lon_deg = 134|lon_min = 56|lon_sec = 51.30 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 24m
Height: 4.7m |burial = Double-entrance {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Earrings, Iron sword, Horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |associated_mounds = |era = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |occupant = |imperial_tomb = |heritage = Kyoto Prefecture Designated Historic Site "Chōjamori Kofun" |notes = |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |type = Circular Kofun |map_dot_label = Chōjamori Kofun |map_dot_label = right }} '''Chōjamori Kofun''' (長者森古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, Yakuno-chō, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and has been Historic Site1 as a Kyoto Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''northwestern Kyoto Prefecture''', near the border with '''Hyōgo Prefecture''', at the tip of a northern hill along the '''Makigawa River''', where the river changes course from south to east. It is situated within the typeer '''{{ill|Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|ja|福知山市立育英小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|1=Fukuchiyama City Ikuei Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594340}}''' grounds. Previously, the site contained a cluster of '''two kofun''', but one mound (Kofun No. 1) was destroyed during school construction, leaving only '''Kofun No. 2''', which is now known as '''Chōjamori Kofun'''.Historical site intypeation board. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The tumulus is '''circular''', measuring '''24 meters in diameter''' and '''4.7 meters in height'''. The burial chamber is a '''double-entrance yokoana-style stone chamber''', opening towards the '''southeast'''. It is a '''large {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 12.2 meters in total length''', making it one of the largest in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture'''. burial goods recovered from the chamber include '''earrings, an iron sword, horse fittings, and Sue pottery'''. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the '''late 6th century''' (MT85 period), with '''multiple secondary burials continuing until the early 7th century''' (TK209 period).{{Sfn|Yakuno’s Late Kofun and Sue Kiln Sites|2024|qq=}} Unlike other regions where large Kofuns appear gradually, '''Chōjamori Kofun was built suddenly, with no prior large kofun in the area'''. The region is a '''strategic transportation hub''', connecting '''Tajima, Tango, Harima, and Tanba''' via the '''{{ill|Maruyama River|en|Maruyama River|qid=Q11394765|lt=Maruyama River|1=Maruyama River}} - {{ill|Ichi_River|en|Ichi_River|qid=Q11480127|lt=Ichi River|1=Ichi River}}''' route and the '''Makigawa - Yura River - {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|en|Kako River (Japan)|qid=Q11398501|lt=Kako River|1=Kako River (Japan)}}''' route, making this kofun significant for understanding the '''ancient political landscape'''. Additionally, across the '''Makigawa River''', a '''series of later chief tombs''' can be found at the '''Chigirituka Kofun Cluster''' and '''Takeuchi Kofun Cluster'''. In '''2002''', the kofun was Historic Site1 a '''Kyoto Prefecture Historic Site'''. == History == * '''1904 (Meiji 37)''' - Kofun No.1 and No.2 discovered during school construction. '''Kofun No.1 was destroyed''', while '''Kofun No.2 remained''' and burial goods were recovered.{{Sfn|Yakuno’s Late Kofun and Sue Kiln Sites|2024|qq=}} * '''1956 (Shōwa 31)''' - Mentioned in *Amata District Nakayakuno Village Historical Records* (unpublished manuscript), along with '''the legend of Chōja Mansion''', linked to the '''Yakuno clan'''.{{Sfn|Yakuno’s Late Kofun and Sue Kiln Sites|2024|qq=}} * '''1975 (Shōwa 50)''' - Published in *Kyoto Archaeology* Vol. 16, with '''first recorded stone chamber survey drawings'''.{{Sfn|Yakuno’s Late Kofun and Sue Kiln Sites|2024|qq=}} * '''1992 (Heisei 4)''' - Designated as a Historic Site by '''Yakuno Town'''. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Published in *Yakuno Town Archaeological map*, featuring '''detailed stone chamber survey drawings'''.{{Sfn|Yakuno’s Late Kofun and Sue Kiln Sites|2024|qq=}} * '''2002 (Heisei 14)''' - Designated a '''Kyoto Prefecture Historic Site''' on '''March 26'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' * '''Vegetation removal''' from the tumulus. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2)''' * '''3D laser scan survey of the stone chamber''' conducted by '''Sogo Giken Co., Ltd.'''. == burial == [[File:Chojagamori Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Chojagamori Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout|qq=}}]] The burial chamber is a '''double-entrance {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}''', facing '''southeast'''. Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|Yakuno Town History Vol. 2|2006|qq=}} * '''Total chamber length''': '''12.2 meters''' * '''Main chamber (gen-shitsu)''': '''5.5m (L) × 2.3m (W) × 2.6m (H)''' * '''Entrance passage (sendō)''': '''6.7m (L) × 1.1m (W at gen-mon)''' The stone materials are '''locally sourced basalt'''.{{Sfn|Yakuno Town History Vol. 2|2006|qq=}} The '''base layer consists of 1-meter-long large stones''', with '''6-7 rows of smaller stones stacked above'''. The chamber walls display '''distinct inward inclination from the 4th-5th layers'''. The '''entrance pillar stones''' are '''rectangular columns''', but '''do not reach the ceiling'''. '''Recovered burial goods''' include: * '''1 earring''' * '''1 iron sword''' * '''Horse fittings''' (fragments of '''iron ring bit''') * '''11 Sue pottery vessels''' (4 lids, 7 bowls){{Sfn|Yakuno’s Late Kofun and Sue Kiln Sites|2024|qq=}}. Chojagamori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Chojagamori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Chojagamori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (toward opening) Chojagamori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (toward main chamber) Chojagamori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Tomb entrance Chojagamori Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Chōjamori Kofun''' - Designated on '''March 26, 2002'''. == Related facilities == * '''Yakuno Fossil and Local History Museum''' (Fukuchiyama City, Yakuno-chō Hirano) - Houses burial goods from '''Chōjamori Kofun'''. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Chojagamori Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.city.fukuchiyama.lg.jp/soshiki/7/1303.html Chōjamori Kofun - Fukuchiyama City Official Website] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|長者森古墳|version=101381659|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:長者森古墳]] {{Commons category|Chojagamori Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q121874531}} [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府指定史跡]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:福知山市の歴史]]
